title: A computational rule-based model of MAPK/ERK system regulation
task: Model differential isoform regulation and 14-3-3 interactions in MAPK/ERK signaling
key_results: Rule-based model reconciles graded RAS/RAF responses with switch-like ERK activation; 14-3-3 dimers inhibit RAF monomers and preferentially stabilize BRAF-CRAF/BRAF-ARAF dimers
comments: The localized RAS activation hypothesis is plausible but parameter sensitivity and experimental validation at single-cell resolution would strengthen predictive confidence.

model_io.model: MAPK/ERK signaling cascade with RAF isoforms and 14-3-3 binding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Localized RAS activation at low growth factor concentrations enables graded response at top tier but switch-like response at ERK
hypotheses.result: Model reconciles observed dual response modes through spatial RAS constraint

hypotheses.hypothesis: 14-3-3 dimers enforce isoform-specific functional differences via selective dimer stabilization
hypotheses.result: Rule-based model predicts 14-3-3 inhibits RAF monomers but stabilizes BRAF-containing heterodimers preferentially

title: Understanding the underlying language code that governs the π–π non-covalent interactions between proteins and DNA
task: Model π–π stacking geometry between aromatic amino acids and DNA bases from first principles
key_results: Parallel displacement geometry is most stable with pz orbitals of purines/pyrimidines interacting with C1–C2 carbons of benzene rings
comments: The electron-pair oscillatory resonant quantum state model is abstract; experimental validation through spectroscopy or crystallography would strengthen mechanistic claims.

model_io.model: Protein-DNA aromatic dimer (amino acid benzene ring + DNA base)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parallel displacement alignment maximizes aromatic coupling stability
hypotheses.result: Theoretical analysis identified parallel displacement as most stable geometry with specific pz orbital overlaps

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dipole coupling enhances quantum correlations in π–π interactions
hypotheses.result: Model predicts enhanced quantum correlations through dipole coupling effects

title: Quasi-elastic neutron scattering studies on bacterial spores and their hydration water
task: Elucidate water dynamics and protein immobility in dormant bacterial spores
key_results: Spores exhibit strongly reduced proteome rotational dynamics but high water mobility at sub-nanosecond timescale despite reduced hydration
comments: The contrast between protein rigidity and water fluidity is intriguing but the mechanistic link to dormancy vigilance remains speculative without direct metabolic validation.

model_io.model: Whole bacterial spores and component-subtracted water signal
inputs: neutron scattering spectra of intact spores

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reduced hydration paired with high water mobility enables dormant spore vigilance
hypotheses.result: Quasi-elastic neutron scattering revealed restricted protein motion but enhanced water molecule mobility, supporting a dynamic dormancy model

title: Unstructured, disulfide-bridged C-terminus in helminth α-helical antimicrobial peptides enhances and modulates their activity
task: Characterize how disulfide-bridged C-terminal tail modulates antimicrobial peptide activity
key_results: Disulfide-bonded mesco-2 forms assemblies and disrupts membranes faster than reduced mesco-2 A; both show strong antibacterial activity with low cytotoxicity
comments: The cyclic tail acts as a tunable element for controlling oligomerization state and kinetics, suggesting C-terminal motifs merit broader attention in AMP design.

model_io.model: mesco-2 and mesco-2 A (flatworm antimicrobial peptides)
inputs: C-terminal disulfide-forming CLGRC motif (mesco-2) or reduced variant (mesco-2 A)

hypotheses.hypothesis: C-terminal disulfide bridge modulates peptide flexibility and oligomerization
hypotheses.result: Molecular modelling and microscale thermophoresis confirmed distinct assembly and binding modes between oxidized and reduced forms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disulfide bond controls speed and cooperativity of membrane disruption
hypotheses.result: AFM and flow cytometry revealed mesco-2 disrupts membranes faster with more cooperative kinetics than mesco-2 A

title: Lactylation stabilizes PD-L1 to promote tumor immune evasion and cell growth
task: Elucidate lactate-mediated PD-L1 stabilization mechanism and its role in tumor immune evasion
key_results: Lactate induces K280 lactylation of PD-L1 by AARS1, blocking HUWE1-mediated ubiquitination; lactylation correlates with advanced NSCLC stages and poor survival
comments: Novel metabolic post-translational modification linking tumor microenvironment lactate to checkpoint inhibitor resistance, with potential therapeutic implications for combination immunotherapy.

model_io.model: tumor cell and CD8+ T cell co-culture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactate stabilizes PD-L1 via lactylation-mediated inhibition of HUWE1-ubiquitination
hypotheses.result: K280 lactylation blocks E3 ligase HUWE1 binding and proteasomal degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: PD-L1 lactylation impairs CD8+ T cell-mediated cytotoxicity
hypotheses.result: Lactylated PD-L1 promotes tumor immune evasion and accelerates growth in vivo

title: TSP-1 interaction with RANK and OPG: implications for bone remodeling and osteolytic bone metastasis
task: Characterize TSP-1 C-terminal fragment regulation of osteoclast differentiation in bone metastasis
key_results: E123CaG TSP-1 fragment inhibits RANKL-induced osteoclast differentiation by blocking RANK signaling; in vivo expression reduces osteolytic lesions and extends survival
comments: Elegant dual-mechanism feedback control where proteolytic cleavage generates an inhibitory fragment that also stabilizes the decoy receptor OPG, representing a sophisticated anti-osteolytic defense.

hypotheses.hypothesis: E123CaG binds RANK and blocks downstream MAPK and NFATc1 signaling
hypotheses.result: RANKL-induced osteoclast differentiation is inhibited

hypotheses.hypothesis: E123CaG stabilizes OPG by protecting it from protease degradation
hypotheses.result: Decoy receptor activity is potentiated, further suppressing osteoclastogenesis

title: An epithelial morphogenetic program for maximal urine concentration
task: Identify epithelial morphogenetic mechanisms enabling maximal urine concentration in mammals
key_results: Claudin-10b drives apical-junctional interdigitations in ascending thin limb; claudin-10b deletion abolishes interdigitations and reduces urine-concentrating ability by ~50%
comments: Elegant integration of single-nucleus transcriptomics with high-resolution imaging reveals a non-canonical morphogenetic role for a canonical tight junction protein, linking nanoscale architecture to whole-organ function.

model_io.model: ascending thin limb epithelium in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Claudin-10b is necessary for apical-junctional interdigitation formation
hypotheses.result: Claudin-10b deletion abolishes interdigitations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interdigitations increase cell-to-cell contact area required for salt reabsorption
hypotheses.result: Claudin-10b deletion markedly reduces maximal urine concentration

title: Validation of ferroptosis in zebrafish as a reliable model for phenotypic studies
task: Validate ferroptosis as a distinct cell death pathway in zebrafish and characterize its impact on angiogenesis
key_results: Ferroptosis inducers (Erastin, FIN56) trigger ROS accumulation, GSH/GSSG ratio alterations, and ferroptosis-related gene expression distinct from apoptotic/autophagic markers; ferroptosis significantly inhibits angiogenesis
comments: Zebrafish validation provides a transparent vertebrate model with 85% human genetic homology; vascular impairment phenotype may have broader developmental and pathological relevance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis can be reliably modeled in zebrafish embryos
hypotheses.result: Ferroptosis inducers trigger iron-dependent cell death distinct from apoptosis and autophagy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis impairs angiogenesis during early development
hypotheses.result: Ferroptosis-induced vascular development inhibition observed in transgenic zebrafish

title: SHP2 improves ovarian morphology and steroidogenic function in a rat PCOS model by modulating IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3-mediated granulosa cell pyroptosis
task: Characterize SHP2-mediated attenuation of granulosa cell pyroptosis in PCOS via IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 signaling
key_results: SHP2 overexpression reduces PCOS-induced ovarian pathology; SHP2 inhibits testosterone-activated IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 and ZEB1 pathways, suppressing GSDMD-mediated granulosa cell pyroptosis and restoring hormonal balance
comments: Multi-level mechanistic investigation connecting phosphatase regulation to endoplasmic reticulum stress and pyroptosis in reproductive endocrinology; therapeutic SHP2 activation represents a novel PCOS intervention strategy.

model_io.model: letrozole-induced PCOS rat model with lentiviral SHP2 overexpression

hypotheses.hypothesis: Testosterone inhibits SHP2 phosphorylation and activates IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 pathway
hypotheses.result: SHP2 overexpression blocks testosterone-induced granulosa cell pyroptosis

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHP2 regulates ZEB1/PKP3 pathway controlling granulosa cell proliferation
hypotheses.result: SHP2 restoration improves ovarian morphology and hormone levels in PCOS

title: Hibernating bear serum triggers an anti-fibrotic signature in human fibroblasts, involving ECM remodeling and MAPK signaling activation
task: Identify anti-fibrotic gene expression programs in human fibroblasts triggered by hibernating bear serum factors
key_results: Hibernating bear serum induces ECM remodeling with reduced collagen (COL2A1, COL11A1, COL9A1) and TGFBI expression; MAPK cascade activation and decreased inflammatory markers (IFI16, HMGB2, HMGB) characterize anti-fibrotic signature
comments: Intriguing cross-species proteomics approach identifies hibernation-adapted circulating factors as tools to interrogate fibrosis resistance; mechanism likely involves integration of metabolic and inflammatory cues.

model_io.model: human fibroblast culture with serum treatment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hibernating bear serum contains factors suppressing fibrotic gene expression
hypotheses.result: Collagen and TGFBI levels reduced; anti-fibrotic signature activated in treated fibroblasts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hibernation-derived factors modulate MAPK signaling and ECM homeostasis
hypotheses.result: MAPK cascade activation correlates with reduced fibrotic and inflammatory protein expression

title: Targeting STAT3 pathway attenuates macrophages inflammation and cardiovascular injury in a model of Kawasaki disease
task: Investigate STAT3 pathway role in macrophage inflammation and cardiovascular injury in Kawasaki disease
key_results: STAT3 phosphorylation upregulated in LCWE-treated macrophages; AG490 inhibition reduces inflammatory cytokine production and attenuates cardiac inflammation and vascular injury in LCWE-KD mouse model
comments: Straightforward in vitro to in vivo validation establishing STAT3 as a druggable target; clinical translatability enhanced by use of standard inhibitor AG490.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LCWE activates STAT3 signaling in macrophages
hypotheses.result: Pharmacological STAT3 inhibition suppresses inflammatory response

hypotheses.hypothesis: Macrophage STAT3 signaling mediates endothelial cell damage
hypotheses.result: Blocking STAT3 reduces conditioned medium-induced endothelial injury and in vivo cardiac lesions

title: mRNA profiling of mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosomes reveals their function in accelerating wound healing
task: Profile mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosome transcriptome and validate wound healing therapeutic efficacy
key_results: UCMSC-derived exosomes contain 4,578 protein-coding genes with 2,004 upregulated; exosome-enriched genes associated with DNA replication, ribosome function, and cell cycle regulation; UCMSC-EX treatment accelerates wound closure in burned mouse model
comments: Transcriptomic signature predicting wound healing function is validated through cellular uptake and in vivo efficacy, supporting selective mRNA packaging as a mechanism of exosome-mediated regenerative therapy.

model_io.model: human dermal fibroblast and keratinocyte culture

hypotheses.hypothesis: UCMSC-derived exosomes selectively enrich wound-healing associated transcripts
hypotheses.result: mRNA profile predicts and correlates with wound healing efficacy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosome uptake by dermal fibroblasts promotes proliferation and migration
hypotheses.result: In vitro fibroblast and keratinocyte responses translate to accelerated wound closure in vivo

title: β-arrestin1 orchestrates endosomal signaling to regulate translational control of circadian light entrainment
task: Elucidate β-arrestin1-dependent endosomal signaling in circadian light entrainment via translational control
key_results: ARRB1 mediates PAC1 receptor internalization and endosomal ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 cascade activation; ARRB1-dependent translational control increases PER protein induction independently of transcriptional responses
comments: Paradigm-shifting demonstration that endosomes function as critical subcellular signaling hubs for circadian entrainment, establishing spatial specificity of GPCR signaling beyond traditional plasma membrane paradigms.

model_io.model: SCN slice preparation with translational monitoring

hypotheses.hypothesis: ARRB1 is required for PAC1 internalization and endosomal signaling activation
hypotheses.result: Light-induced ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 cascade depends on ARRB1-mediated endosomal signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Endosomal ERK signaling enhances PER translation independent of transcription
hypotheses.result: ARRB1-dependent translational control contributes to circadian entrainment

title: Multi-omic identification of key transcriptional regulatory programs during endurance exercise training in rats
task: Multi-omic identification of transcriptional regulators driving endurance exercise training adaptations
key_results: Integrated analysis of chromatin accessibility, DNA methylation, mRNA expression, protein abundance, and phosphorylation across eight tissues in 50 rats; identified tissue-specific transcription factor motifs enriched in differentially expressed genes, accessible regions, and methylated regions; distinct regulatory routes: epigenomic (improving TF accessibility) vs. TF expression/activity changes
comments: Comprehensive multi-omic integration reveals coordinated epigenomic-transcriptomic regulation; tissue specificity and dual regulatory mechanisms (epigenomic vs. TF-centric) add mechanistic depth.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Endurance training induces coordinated epigenomic and transcriptional changes mediated by tissue-specific transcription factors
hypotheses.result: TF motif enrichment identified in DEGs, accessible regions, and methylated regions; tissue-specific patterns revealed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Training-induced regulation occurs via two distinct routes: epigenomic alterations improving TF accessibility, and TF expression/activity changes
hypotheses.result: Multi-omic analysis identified both regulatory mechanisms; TF target genes correlated with exercise phenotypes and cell-type composition changes

title: Investigating the mechanistic link between pesticide DDT and breast cancer through network toxicology, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation
task: Mechanistic elucidation of DDT-breast cancer link via network toxicology and molecular docking
key_results: Identified 12 DDT-breast cancer target genes; core targets (AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2) involved in hormone/growth-factor signaling; network and docking analyses clarified molecular mechanisms
comments: Network toxicology identifies plausible pathways but lacks experimental validation; findings remain computational and require epidemiological follow-up.

inputs: breast cancer protein targets (AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2)

hypotheses.hypothesis: DDT exerts breast cancer risk through hormone and growth-factor signaling pathways mediated by AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2
hypotheses.result: Network toxicology and docking identified these as core targets; pathway analysis confirmed hormone/growth-factor involvement

title: Feature reduction using swarm optimization and random forest classifiers for early diabetes risk prediction
task: Early diabetes risk prediction via swarm-optimization feature selection and random forest classifiers
key_results: TSO_RF (Tuna Swarm Optimization + Random Forest) achieved 100% accuracy without cross-validation and 98.14% mean 10-fold CV accuracy using only 14 of 16 features; F-score 98.47%, Precision 98.54%; SHAP identified Polyuria, Polydipsia, Gender as top predictive features
comments: Exceptional feature reduction (14/16 features) without performance loss; SHAP provides interpretability; uncross-validated 100% accuracy requires scrutiny for data leakage.

inputs: Early Stage Diabetes Risk Prediction dataset (16 features, 520 samples)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Swarm-based metaheuristic feature selection combined with Random Forest achieves high accuracy with minimal feature set
hypotheses.result: TSO_RF achieved 100% and 98.14% accuracy (without and with 10-fold CV) using 14 features; HBA_RF reduced to 10 features with moderate performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polyuria, Polydipsia, and Gender are the most critical predictors of early-stage diabetes risk
hypotheses.result: SHAP ranked these three features as highest importance; individual prediction analysis confirmed strong influence on model decisions

title: A multi-scale adaptive filtering and AtRes_SRU–transformer synergy for breast cancer histopathology classification
task: Breast cancer histopathology classification with multi-scale adaptive filtering and transformer networks
key_results: ImTranNet-TriCore achieved 95.92% overall accuracy; 97.83% precision on Brain MRI, 97.67% on Chest X-ray; outperformed CNNs, RNNs, and standalone transformers on noisy biomedical data
comments: While performance metrics are strong, the paper combines multiple modalities (histopathology, MRI, X-ray) but lacks clarity on dataset splits and potential overfitting risk.

inputs: biomedical images (histopathology, Brain MRI, Chest X-ray)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-scale adaptive filtering, dual-path attentive residual units, and hybrid transformers synergistically improve classification on complex, noisy biomedical images
hypotheses.result: ImTranNet-TriCore achieved 95.92% accuracy and outperformed baseline deep learning architectures across modalities

title: A novel bioenergetic model outlines the metabolism of a deep-sea clam and that of its sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
task: Bioenergetic modeling of deep-sea chemosymbiotic clam and sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
key_results: Two Dynamic Energy Budget models developed; innovative 'farming' model explicitly models symbiont digestion; host exhibits homeostatic control of ingestion rate despite lower-than-maximal intake; adult clam oxygen consumption predominantly by symbionts; high host energy maintenance flux inferred from ion homeostasis demands
comments: Novel DEB application to chemosymbiosis reveals unexpected metabolic strategies (homeostatic ingestion control); symbiont contribution to oxygen consumption is a significant mechanistic insight.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Host forages at below-maximal ingestion rate to maintain stable metabolic homeostasis with symbionts
hypotheses.result: Farming model predicted host homeostatic ingestion control, a novel regulatory strategy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adult clam oxygen consumption is dominated by chemosynthetic symbionts rather than host metabolism
hypotheses.result: Model predicted majority of oxygen consumed by symbionts in adult state

title: Hybrid tuned deep learning model for breast cancer diagnosis using genetic data
task: Breast cancer diagnosis and mortality prediction from genomic data
key_results: Achieved 99.30% accuracy and 100% recall on METABRIC validation using CNN-BiLSTM hybrid; feature selection reduced 17,814 genes to optimal biomarker set via Random Forest and Association Rule Mining
comments: The extremely high performance metrics warrant independent external validation given the risk of overfitting on high-dimensional genomic data despite stated augmentation strategies.

inputs: whole-genome expression (17,814 genes TCGA; 503 features METABRIC)

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-dimensional genomic integration improves breast cancer prediction over curated gene sets
hypotheses.result: CNN-BiLSTM achieved 97.4% accuracy on TCGA and 99.3% on METABRIC; ARM+RF feature selection identified critical biomarkers

title: A novel spatiotemporal decomposition and identification of sparse equations for human brain deformation
task: Discovery of sparse governing equations for human brain deformation dynamics
key_results: TASC-SINDy model reduced dimensionality to three generic modes capturing complete brain deformation dynamics; accurately predicted strain fields in nine held-out test subjects from 4D tagged MRI data
comments: Data-driven equation discovery from in vivo tagged MRI is elegant and achieves exceptional compression; clinical utility for prediction/intervention planning is promising but untested.

inputs: 4D spatiotemporal strain data (tagged MRI from 36 subjects)

outputs: parsimonious set of governing equations for brain deformation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brain deformation dynamics are governed by sparse, low-dimensional equations discoverable from tagged MRI via TASC-SINDy
hypotheses.result: Three-mode TASC-SINDy model accurately predicted held-out test subjects' strain fields with exceptional dimensionality reduction

title: Development and validation of an interpretable prediction model for the risk of unplanned reoperation in patients underwent intracranial tumor surgery
task: Interpretable prediction of unplanned reoperation risk in intracranial tumor surgery patients
key_results: Logistic regression model selected from five ML approaches; achieved AUC 0.836 (training) and 0.769 (internal validation); 11 key variables identified via LASSO; SHAP analysis ranked surgery duration, tumor location, Frailty Index-5, and tumor type as most important
comments: Single-center internal validation limits generalizability; web-based tool deployment enhances clinical utility but requires prospective external validation.

model_io.model: Logistic Regression (LR) + four alternative ML models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Machine learning can accurately predict unplanned reoperation risk in intracranial tumor surgery using readily available clinical/surgical variables
hypotheses.result: LR achieved AUC 0.836 (training) and 0.769 (validation); SHAP identified surgery duration, tumor location, Frailty Index-5, tumor type as top predictors

title: Performance of a GPU- and time-efficient pseudo-3D network for magnetic resonance image super-resolution and motion artifact reduction
task: GPU-efficient pseudo-3D deep learning for MRI super-resolution and motion artifact reduction
key_results: TS-RCAN (2D network for pseudo-3D processing) outperformed 3D networks by >0.01 dB SSIM / 1.5 dB PSNR; reduced GPU load and inference time by up to 90%; optimal down-sampling factors identified (1×1×2 for ×2, 2×2×2 for ×4 acceleration)
comments: Practical efficiency gains (90% GPU reduction) combined with superior image quality make this clinically deployable; uncertainty estimation for quality prediction without ground truth is valuable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2D networks operating on optimally down-sampled 3D data achieve pseudo-3D super-resolution and motion artifact reduction more efficiently than full 3D networks
hypotheses.result: TS-RCAN outperformed 3D networks on SSIM/PSNR while reducing GPU/inference time by 90%; down-sampling factors 1×1×2 (×2 accel) and 2×2×2 (×4 accel) optimal

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uncertainty estimates correlate with image quality metrics, enabling accuracy prediction without ground truth
hypotheses.result: Uncertainty module successfully predicted quality metrics, supporting clinical deployment feasibility

title: The most important features in generalized additive models might be groups of features
task: Feature importance assessment for groups in generalized additive models
key_results: Developed efficient group importance method for GAMs requiring no model retraining; identified that group features (not individual features) are often the strongest predictors in multimodal neuroscience and clinical datasets
comments: The post-hoc grouping flexibility and applicability to high-dimensional settings are valuable, though computational efficiency details are sparse.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Groups of related features provide joint signal that surpasses individual feature importance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via synthetic experiments and two clinical case studies (depression phenotyping, social determinants in hip arthroplasty)

title: Classification of fallers and non-fallers in older adults using electrical IMU signal for gait analysis and explainable deep learning
task: Classification of fall risk in older adults from IMU gait signals via explainable deep learning
key_results: CNN achieved 92% fall-classification accuracy under stringent subject-wise cross-validation (Experiment 2); LSTM achieved 95% under data-level CV (Experiment 1); LIME analysis revealed stance-phase irregularities (83.8% fallers vs. 61.7% non-fallers) drive classification, linked to impaired weight transfer and balance
comments: Stride-level gait signal classification with explainability is rigorous; subject-wise validation ensures generalization; prospective longitudinal studies needed to validate predictive utility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning on raw IMU stride time-series accurately classifies fall history in older adults with superior generalization
hypotheses.result: CNN achieved 92% accuracy under subject-wise validation; robust across age strata (70-99 years)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stance-phase gait irregularities are the primary driver of fall-history classification and correlate with known fall-risk factors
hypotheses.result: LIME revealed 83.8% contribution of stance irregularities in fallers vs. 61.7% in non-fallers; terminal/mid-stance deficits linked to weight transfer/balance impairment

title: Hair and artifact removal in dermoscopic images using deep learning for enhanced skin cancer detection
task: Hair and artifact removal in dermoscopic images for improved melanoma detection
key_results: U-Net framework improved PSNR from 21.5 to 34.1 dB and SSIM from 0.79 to 0.89 (HAM10000); SSIM enhanced to 0.92 on ISIC 2018; downstream melanoma classification accuracy improved from 84.2% to 90.3–91.5%; robust generalization across artifact types and lesion diversity
comments: Significant preprocessing-driven improvements in downstream classification; hybrid Dice/BCE loss and extensive augmentation drive robustness; IoU 0.85–0.87 indicates reliable artifact/hair segmentation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: U-Net-based artifact removal preserves lesion features (pigmentary networks, irregular edges) while eliminating hair and reflections
hypotheses.result: PSNR improved 21.5→34.1 dB; SSIM 0.79→0.89 (HAM10000); melanoma classification accuracy improved 84.2%→90.3–91.5%

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preprocessing via artifact removal generalizes across diverse artifact types and lesion morphologies
hypotheses.result: Robust performance on both HAM10000 and ISIC 2018; generalization confirmed across imaging environments

title: Q-CaDD: accelerating in silico methodologies with quantum computation and machine learning for Epidermal growth factor receptor
task: Accelerated drug discovery for EGFR via hybrid quantum-classical machine learning
key_results: Q-CaDD framework generated 200,000 ligands; Quantum SVM ensemble achieved AUC-ROC 0.83 on Tox21 NR-AR pathway; identified candidate molecules with favorable affinity and toxicity profiles
comments: Hybrid quantum-classical approach is innovative but constrained by NISQ hardware limitations; modest AUC improvement over classical baselines warrants further optimization.

model_io.model: Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) + classical ensemble

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum-enhanced machine learning components provide complementary predictive signals in hybrid drug discovery workflows
hypotheses.result: QSVM ensemble achieved AUC-ROC 0.83; preliminary candidates identified with favorable affinity/toxicity; performance competitive with classical benchmarks

title: Construction of a diagnostic model for preeclampsia based on differentially expressed lactylation-related genes and the immune infiltration analysis
task: Diagnostic biomarker discovery for preeclampsia via lactylation-related genes
key_results: Identified two lactylation-related DEGs (EAF1, PFKP) significantly upregulated in preeclampsia; logistic regression model achieved 81.7% accuracy on GSE54618 training and 91% on validation; pathway enrichment linked to Glycolysis/Gluconeogenesis
comments: Single-cell RNA-seq adds cell-type resolution and validates candidate genes in placental tissue, strengthening mechanistic inference.

outputs: two distinct preeclampsia subtypes with immune profiles

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactylation-related genes drive preeclampsia pathogenesis through Glycolysis/Gluconeogenesis pathway
hypotheses.result: EAF1 and PFKP significantly upregulated; pathway enrichment confirmed; diagnostic model achieved 91% validation accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preeclampsia heterogeneity reflects distinct immune infiltration profiles
hypotheses.result: NMF identified two subtypes with significantly different immune cell compositions

title: AI-based detection of Certas Plus shunt valve settings in CT scans
task: AI-based automatic detection of adjustable CSF shunt valve settings in CT scans
key_results: 3D U-Net successfully segmented valve components in 97.3% of test cases; correctly predicted exact or adjacent valve setting in 96% of cases; enables clinician interpretation via visualized segmentations
comments: High accuracy and interpretability via segmentation outputs make this clinically viable; limited to one valve model (Certas Plus) may restrict generalization.

inputs: head CT scans with Certas Plus CSF shunt

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D U-Net can reliably segment valve components and infer settings from CT imaging despite metal artifacts and limited spatial resolution
hypotheses.result: Achieved 97.3% segmentation and 96% setting prediction accuracy; segmentations enable clinician verification

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence in intensive care units: from narrow tools to generalist systems
task: Regulatory framework for AI in intensive care units across paradigms of complexity
key_results: Proposed five-paradigm framework mapping rising regulatory complexity with AI functionality/scale; identifies shift from narrow device-centric models to generalist systems and agentic AI
comments: Conceptual framework for AI governance in ICUs; lacks quantitative metrics or empirical data on regulatory outcomes, serving primarily as a policy perspective.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Regulatory complexity must scale with AI system functionality and scope (narrow to generalist, single-device to multi-agent orchestration)
hypotheses.result: Five-paradigm framework proposed; agentic oversight needed for orchestrating complex AI systems

title: Evolutionary digital twin framework for optimal aminoglycoside dosing in neonates with suspected sepsis
task: Optimal aminoglycoside dosing in neonatal sepsis via digital twins
key_results: Multiscale digital twins calibrated on 1,634 neonates; nonlinear optimal control achieved bacteriostatic exposure across all simulated patients; model predictive control reduced bacterial rebound during late therapy
comments: Integration of physiologically-based pharmacokinetics with eco-evolutionary dynamics and LSTM-updated GFR is innovative; real-world clinical validation remains needed.

model_io.model: Physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) model + eco-evolutionary pharmacodynamic module
Nonlinear optimal control + Model Predictive Control

hypotheses.hypothesis: Digital twins accounting for physiological diversity and resistance evolution enable safer precision dosing
hypotheses.result: Nonlinear optimal control achieved bacteriostatic exposure in all digital neonates; MPC reduced bacterial rebound in late-phase therapy

title: High-sensitivity pan-cancer AI assessment of lymph node metastasis via uncertainty quantification
task: Pan-cancer AI lymph node metastasis assessment with uncertainty quantification to reduce false negatives
key_results: UPATHLN achieved AUC 0.986 on internal validation; uncertainty module flagged potential false-negative predictions for mandatory pathologist review, intercepting 100% of missed diagnoses across development and independent test cohorts, including seven unseen primary origins; reduced negative lymph node review burden by 73.2%
comments: Safety-critical uncertainty quantification is exemplary for clinical deployment; 100% conditional sensitivity under uncertainty-triggered review is exceptional; generalization to unseen primary origins validates approach.

model_io.model: Pathology foundation model encoder + decoupled uncertainty estimation module
inputs: lymph node histopathology images (26,229 samples, 14 primary origins)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit uncertainty modeling prevents 'overconfident errors' and intercepts false-negative metastasis predictions
hypotheses.result: Uncertainty module achieved 100% conditional sensitivity across development and independent test cohorts; reduced false negatives to zero

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uncertainty quantification improves diagnostic safety while reducing pathologist review burden on negative cases
hypotheses.result: Intercepted all missed diagnoses; reduced negative review burden by 73.2%

title: Additive-mediated interfacial engineering of H2SO4-catalyzed isobutane alkylation from molecular design to industrial process intensification
task: Design interfacial mass transfer engineering for H₂SO₄-catalyzed isobutane alkylation
key_results: PPG400 additive increases alkylate capacity 24.85% to 99.83 kt/a (vs 79.96 kt/a additive-free); CFD-PBM model quantifies microscopic-mesoscopic coupling; reaction mass-transfer controlled
comments: Multiscale approach bridging molecular design to industrial process; interfacial enhancement factor E provides rational additive screening criterion.

outputs: E ratio for optimal additive selection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Additive-mediated interfacial engineering improves alkylate production
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PPG400 increases capacity 24.85%

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reaction is mass-transfer controlled
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Nisobutane decoupling validates control regime

title: Revealing the intrinsic ethical vulnerability of aligned large language models
task: Analyze vulnerability of aligned LLMs to adversarial attacks
key_results: 100% attack success rate on 22/26 state-of-the-art LLMs; theoretical proof that alignment methods create only localized safety regions
comments: Important security finding but relies on distributional shift assumptions that may not reflect all threat models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Harmful knowledge persists as dark patterns despite alignment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 100% attack success rate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Alignment creates only localized safety regions
hypotheses.result: Theoretically proven and empirically validated

title: Wearable optomyography enables continuous neuroprosthetic control
task: Enable continuous neuroprosthetic control via wearable optomyography
key_results: 50-channel OMG wristband outperforms traditional sEMG; 8 able-bodied + 1 limb-loss participant mastered mouse-like tasks and Tetris
comments: Promising alternative to sEMG with better stability; small sample size (n=9) and lack of quantitative performance metrics vs. sEMG limit generalizability claims.

outputs: computer mouse commands (cursor control, target acquisition)

hypotheses.hypothesis: OMG overcomes sEMG limitations (fixation, cross-talk, artifacts)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via successful mastery of tasks by participants

hypotheses.hypothesis: Compact neural network suffices for OMG decoding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; efficient architecture demonstrated

title: Rapid earthquake damage assessment via hybrid LSTM-RNN with a quantum-inspired classification head based on Autonomous Perceptron Model APM
task: Rapid earthquake damage assessment via hybrid LSTM-RNN with quantum-inspired classification
key_results: LSTM-RNN outperforms stacked LSTM: R² 99.42% (displacement), 97.58% (velocity), 99.8% (acceleration), 99.97% (Damage Index); APM head offers quantum-inspired alternative
comments: Comprehensive multi-task framework with strong regression results; quantum-inspired APM lacks clear motivation over standard Softmax, and computational benefit unclear.

outputs: displacement u, velocity v, acceleration a, Damage Index DI

hypotheses.hypothesis: LSTM-RNN better captures temporal dependencies than stacked LSTM
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior R² and error metrics across all tasks

hypotheses.hypothesis: APM head provides compact damage classification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed but advantage over Softmax not quantified

title: PrivEdge: a hybrid split–federated learning framework for real-time electricity theft detection on edge nodes
task: Detect electricity theft on edge devices via hybrid split-federated learning
key_results: Higher accuracy and F1-score than centralized/FL-only/SL-only baselines, especially in non-IID settings; ≤90% reduction in GPU load and inference time on Raspberry Pi 4
comments: Practical edge deployment with privacy guarantees (ε=3), though ε value is relatively permissive for strong differential privacy.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Split-federated learning reduces communication costs vs. raw data transmission
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; intermediate activations replace raw data

hypotheses.hypothesis: Non-IID data handling improves in hybrid SL-FL vs. single paradigm
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in non-IID experiments

title: Reliability analysis in stress-strength model under record values with practical verification
task: Estimate stress-strength reliability via upper record values under inverted exponentiated Pareto distribution
key_results: Bayesian estimators under symmetric loss outperform asymmetric; credible intervals shorter than bootstrap confidence intervals; consistency validated in simulation
comments: Rigorous Bayesian and frequentist framework with real-data validation (European Champions League goals, sulfur dioxide); record-value framework computationally tractable.

inputs: upper record values from strength T and stress Z

hypotheses.hypothesis: Symmetric Bayesian loss yields better precision than asymmetric
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lower estimation error

hypotheses.hypothesis: Credible intervals more efficient than bootstrap CI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; shorter lengths across record numbers

title: Early prediction of wind turbine anomalies using 1D-CNN and temporal feature engineering on multi-source SCADA data
task: Detect wind turbine anomalies via 1D-CNN and hybrid CNN-LSTM with temporal feature engineering
key_results: 1D-CNN achieves 85% accuracy/F1-score; hybrid CNN-LSTM + attention outperforms base models by 2%; robust multi-source SCADA data integration
comments: Strong benchmark across models; temporal feature engineering crucial for generalization; hybrid attention mechanism effectively balances local and temporal patterns.

inputs: multivariate time-series SCADA data (3 wind farms)

hypotheses.hypothesis: 1D-CNN extracts localized temporal patterns better than RNNs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 85% accuracy outperforms recurrent models

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNN-LSTM + attention improves generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2% accuracy gain and better cross-farm transfer

title: A neutrosophic clustering approach to handle recommendation uncertainty for gray sheep users
task: Recommend items to gray sheep users via neutrosophic clustering and collaborative filtering
key_results: Neutrosophic k-means + IBCF achieves Precision 88.70%, Recall 90.90%, F1 89.79% on MovieLens 100K for gray sheep cluster; MAE 0.534, RMSE 0.719, Accuracy 84.07%
comments: Novel use of neutrosophic logic (truth/indeterminacy/falsity) well-motivated for ambiguous user preferences; generalization to Book-Crossing and Last.fm confirms domain transfer.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Neutrosophic logic better captures gray sheep ambiguity than fuzzy clustering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved precision/recall for gray sheep

hypotheses.hypothesis: IBCF tailored to high-indeterminacy cluster improves recommendations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; higher accuracy than standard CF

title: Dynamics of a discrete-time predator-prey model with exponential prey growth and saturated response
task: Analyze bifurcations in discrete predator-prey model with Holling response and Ricker growth
key_results: Period-doubling and Neimark-Sacker bifurcations demonstrated; transition from order to chaos via Ricker map characterized
comments: Rigorous bifurcation analysis with good phase portrait and Lyapunov exponent visualization; ecological relevance noted but not extensively validated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Holling functional response induces period-doubling bifurcation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via bifurcation theory and central manifold theorem

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ricker map generates chaotic dynamics under parameter variation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via numerical simulations

title: A two-stage framework for cost-sensitive predictive maintenance using deep learning, GANs, and risk-aware clustering
task: Optimize maintenance scheduling via RUL prediction, data augmentation, and cost-aware clustering
key_results: Two-stage framework (LSTM RUL + WGAN-GP augmentation + DBSCAN clustering + cost optimization) reduces corrective failures and normalized maintenance costs vs. non-clustering baselines
comments: Practical decision-oriented approach linking RUL to maintenance optimization; WGAN-GP addresses data scarcity effectively; sensitivity analysis shows robustness to cost assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: WGAN-GP data augmentation stabilizes RUL modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved downstream performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: RUL-based clustering enables opportunistic maintenance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced costs vs. fixed/random policies

title: A crisscross-strategy-boosted beaver behavior optimizer for global optimization and oil reservoir production
task: Optimize global and oil reservoir production via enhanced Beaver Behavior Optimizer with crisscross strategy
key_results: CCBBO achieves Friedman rank 1.5517 on CEC2017 (vs. BBO 2.8966); oil reservoir: NPV 9.512×10⁸ USD (vs. 79.96×10⁹ kt/a baseline), std 1.481×10⁷ USD
comments: Horizontal and vertical crossover search effectively promote information exchange; consistent superior performance across benchmarks and real 60-D oil problem.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crisscross strategy reduces BBO stagnation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; rank 1.5517 vs 2.8966

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCBBO robust to high-dimensional complex optima
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent CEC2017 and 60-D oil results

title: On the min-entropy of equilibrium graphs in network creation games
task: Quantify structural complexity of equilibrium graphs in network creation games
key_results: Exact EGME characterization for edge-cost regimes α<2 and α≥2; strong correlation with density, diameter, betweenness centrality
comments: Novel entropy-based measure for network stability; simulation-validated but theoretical characterization could be stronger for α≥2 cases.

hypotheses.hypothesis: EGME distinguishes structural variations induced by parameter changes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulations; strong correlation with standard metrics

hypotheses.hypothesis: EGME correlates with network stability
hypotheses.result: Implied but not directly tested

title: An effective detection model based on YOLO for pore defects in additive manufacturing
task: Detect pore defects in selective laser melting microscopic images
key_results: Excellent performance on custom SLM dataset; automated pixel-proportion quantification of defects; Chebyshev polynomial fitting of process parameters to porosity
comments: Combines image segmentation with polynomial fitting effectively, though dataset size/diversity not specified.

model_io.model: High-performance image segmentation model with attention and feature fusion

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive learning rate and attention modules improve segmentation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by excellent experimental performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chebyshev polynomials capture process-parameter-to-defect relationships
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via in-depth analysis

title: Position tracking control of electro-hydrostatic actuators via adaptive finite-time backstepping and state observation
task: Control electro-hydrostatic actuator position via adaptive finite-time backstepping
key_results: Finite-time stability achieved under unmeasured states and disturbances; neural network adaptive law stabilizes unknown dynamics
comments: Rigorous Lyapunov analysis; both simulation and experimental results validate the control scheme.

model_io.model: Radial basis function neural network (adaptive law)

hypotheses.hypothesis: State observer reconstructs unmeasured EHSC states
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables control synthesis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive NN law stabilizes unknown system dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via Lyapunov analysis and experiments

title: Blockchain-based two-level trustable reputation framework for e-commerce platform using smart contracts
task: Detect e-commerce fraud via blockchain-based reputation framework with deep learning
key_results: Outperforms compared works in reputation classification; redactable blockchain enables transaction modification; multi-factor authentication mitigates impersonation
comments: Combines practical security (MFA, blockchain, smart contracts) with deep learning but lacks quantitative comparison metrics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-factor auth (PIN+OTP+biometric) reduces impersonation attacks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed as implemented mechanism

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep transformer classifies reputation better than traditional ML
hypotheses.result: Implied but not explicitly quantified

title: Projection Kernel regularization for diffusion-based multimodal remote sensing segmentation
task: Segment multimodal remote sensing images via projection-kernel regularized diffusion
key_results: Consistent improvement over CNN/Transformer/diffusion baselines on ISPRS Vaihingen and Potsdam; enhanced global consistency and sharper boundaries
comments: Clever use of projection kernels to avoid numerical sampling; effective CADEF and HERD mechanisms support multiscale refinement.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Projection-kernel regularization enforces global distribution alignment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines in consistency and boundary delineation

hypotheses.hypothesis: CADEF+HERD stabilize multiscale refinement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via ablation studies

title: Choice-induced preference change under a sequential sampling model framework
task: Model choice-induced preference change via drift-diffusion model with dynamic values
key_results: DDM with non-stationary option values reproduces spreading of alternatives (SoA); accounts for relationships between SoA, choice difficulty, attribute disparity, RT
comments: Elegant relaxation of stationarity assumption; empirical validation limited to indirect fit to literature findings rather than direct experimental replication.

model_io.model: Drift-Diffusion Model (DDM) with dynamic attribute evolution

hypotheses.hypothesis: Non-stationary values in DDM generate spreading of alternatives
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; model reproduces empirical SoA patterns

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-attribute evidence necessary for full SoA reproduction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; single-attribute DDM insufficient

title: Dynamic channel allocation for secondary users in cognitive radio network
task: Allocate spectrum dynamically to secondary users in cognitive radio networks via fuzzy logic
key_results: 27-rule Mamdani fuzzy system improves throughput, reduces service delay and drop rate vs. traditional CRN; adaptive SINR/power/capacity-based channel assignment
comments: Distributed fuzzy decision-making well-suited to CRN heterogeneity; energy detection spectrum sensing standard; interweave/underlay hybrid approach practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fuzzy logic adapts channel selection to network dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved throughput vs. fixed policies

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distributed SU decision-making scales better than centralized
hypotheses.result: Implied; not directly tested

title: BGC-LiteNet: BeiDou grid code embedded lightweight neural architecture for real-time UAV fire detection and localization
task: Real-time UAV forest fire detection and geolocation via embedded spatial priors
key_results: 88.9% mAP and 92.4% geolocation accuracy with 0.87M parameters and 38.2 ms latency; robust under low illumination (72.3% mAP), dense smoke (72.6%), small fires (86.7% recall)
comments: Novel integration of BeiDou Grid Code into neural architecture is elegant; latency-aware NAS successfully balances accuracy and deployment constraints.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Embedded geographic priors improve localization without post-processing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 92.4% geolocation accuracy achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Latency-aware NAS enables millisecond inference on edge
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 38.2 ms latency on embedded platforms

title: Memory efficient training for 3D brain image registration networks using PatchMorph
task: Reduce memory demand for 3D brain image registration via patch-based training
key_results: PatchMorph reduces transformer memory from ~40 GB to <10 GB on 256³ voxel images; state-of-the-art registration with modular architecture; higher inference time trade-off
comments: Clever coarse-to-fine patch-based approach with world-coordinate spatial consistency; memory gains substantial but inference slower than single-shot methods.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Patch-based training decouples architecture from voxel resolution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables transformer scaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Coarse-to-fine cascading preserves spatial consistency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; state-of-the-art performance on T1 MRI and marmoset data

title: Analytical analysis of the nonlinear fractional order Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity in elastic materials
task: Solve nonlinear fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity
key_results: Kumar-Malik method yields bright/dark solitons, kink solitons, and solitary waves; fractional parameter χ controls soliton shape, width, and amplitude
comments: Analytical solutions visualized well via Mathematica; conformable fractional derivative is appropriate for memory effects in elastic materials.

inputs: fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity
outputs: analytical soliton solutions (bright, dark, kink, solitary)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conformable fractional derivative captures memory effects in elastic media
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via solution analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractional parameter χ modulates soliton properties nonlinearly
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 2D/3D/contour plots

title: Effects of AI-assisted review presentation formats on consumer decision-making efficiency from a cognitive load perspective
task: Analyze cognitive load effects of AI-driven review formats on movie purchase decisions
key_results: Bullet-point reviews reduce cognitive load 11.8% in high-demand contexts (complex narrative); no difference in low-demand; cognitive load mediates format-choice relationship
comments: Rigorous experimental design (2×2 factorial); cognitive load theory grounded; moderation by task complexity is nuanced but effect size modest.

inputs: movie type (high vs low cognitive demand)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bullet-point reviews reduce cognitive load vs paragraph in high-demand contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 11.8% reduction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cognitive load mediates format-choice relationship
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; moderated mediation significant

title: Development and validation of an AI use scale for sport and exercise science students
task: Develop and validate domain-specific AI literacy scale for sport science students
key_results: 14-item tool with 4 factors (Awareness, Ethics, Trust, Expectations); CFI 1.00, TLI 0.99, RMSEA 0.09; Cronbach's α 0.90–0.94; AVE 0.77–0.87
comments: Rigorous psychometric validation (EFA + CFA on separate samples, n=864); good internal/convergent/discriminant validity; context-specific instrument addresses gap in sport education.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 4-factor structure captures AI literacy dimensions in sport context
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via EFA (KMO 0.95) and CFA (CFI 1.00)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale demonstrates reliability and validity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Cronbach's α 0.90–0.94, AVE 0.77–0.87, HTMT <0.85

title: Skilled personnel risk management under war-induced forced migration: implications for labor market sustainability
task: Forecast workforce risks from war-induced migration using Human Capital Risk Index and Monte Carlo
key_results: Poland HCRI 0.0372, Czechia 0.0419 (highest risk); Germany resilient despite largest refugee numbers; MC simulations reveal non-IID volatility across economies
comments: Novel HCRI metric quantifies labor market pressure; long-term strategic approach integrating repatriation scenarios; limited by self-reported data and simplified assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Smaller economies face higher labor market risk from migration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Poland/Czechia HCRI > Germany

hypotheses.hypothesis: Family reunification benefits aging labor markets
hypotheses.result: Proposed; demographic benefit highlighted but not quantified

title: Physics-based machine learning toolbox for probing concentration under diffusive regime in microfluidics devices
task: Learn concentration dynamics in microfluidics via physics-informed reduced basis and deep learning
key_results: U-Net and Convolutional Autoencoder map experimental images + parameters to concentration profiles; predicts spatio-temporal evolution, effective diffusivities, precipitation locations
comments: Physics-based learning reduces training samples; digital twin application promising but validation on real microfluidics experiments would strengthen claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-based RBM enables learning with limited samples
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; efficient training demonstrated

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning predicts concentration without expensive CFD
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; virtual probes enable real-time analysis

title: Macroscopic particle transport in dissipative long-range bosonic systems
task: Maximal speed and distance of macroscopic particle transport in dissipative long-range bosonic systems
key_results: Optimal transport speed depends on loss mechanism (one-body vs multi-body); arbitrarily small gain enables long-distance transport in dilute systems; decoherence-free subspaces facilitate perfect transport
comments: Rigorous open quantum system formalism provides fundamental bounds, though experimental verification of transport protocols remains to be demonstrated.

model_io.model: Dissipative bosonic lattice system with long-range hopping and interactions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-body loss more severely restricts transport than one-body loss
hypotheses.result: Fundamental distinction in transport scaling established between loss mechanisms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoherence-free subspaces enable long-distance transport despite dissipation
hypotheses.result: DFS subspaces facilitate perfect transport processes confirmed analytically

title: Flat band induced quasi-one-dimensional magnon transport in a two-dimensional spin lattice
task: Demonstration of quasi-1D magnon transport via flat magnonic bands in 2D antiferromagnetic insulator CrOCl
key_results: Magnon diffusion length >7 μm along a-axis matches low-damping 3D magnets (α-Fe2O3, YIG); strong suppression along b-axis; anisotropic transport attributed to flat vs dispersive magnon bands
comments: Elegant exploitation of flat magnon bands for directional transport, though practical integration into spintronic devices and comparison with YIG-based platforms needs further work.

model_io.model: Two-dimensional antiferromagnetic insulator CrOCl with intrinsic flat magnon bands
outputs: band structure (flat vs dispersive along directions)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nearly flat magnonic band along b-axis suppresses magnon diffusion; dispersive band along a-axis enables long-range transport
hypotheses.result: Anisotropic diffusion lengths measured and confirmed by band structure calculations; quasi-1D transport demonstrated

title: An ultrafast plenoptic-camera system for high-resolution 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators
task: 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators using plenoptic imaging
key_results: Spatial resolution of 200 micrometers; full event reconstruction for neutrino detection demonstrated
comments: Promising alternative to expensive segmented detectors, though single-photon reconstruction in large volumes remains experimentally challenging.

model_io.model: Plenoptic camera with time-resolving SPAD array

hypotheses.hypothesis: Plenoptic systems can enable high-resolution 3D imaging without dense segmentation
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated 200 μm spatial resolution in unsegmented scintillator

title: Supercurrent-controlled spontaneous ferromagnetism of magnetic impurities in a spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
task: Ferromagnetic ordering of magnetic impurities in spin-orbit-coupled superconductor via supercurrent control
key_results: Long-range ferromagnetic order induced on Fe(Se,Te) surface; magnetic switching current density ~1000× lower than ferromagnetic metals; supercurrent-controlled spontaneous magnetization
comments: Novel magneto-electric effect in Rashba superconductor enables ultraefficient magnetic switching, though mechanism of ferromagnetic impurity state formation requires deeper understanding.

model_io.model: Fe(Se,Te) surface with Rashba spin-orbit coupling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Rashba SOC enables ferromagnetic coupling in s-wave superconductor despite spin-singlet pairing
hypotheses.result: Spontaneous ferromagnetic order observed via SQUID microscopy on Fe(Se,Te) surface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magneto-electric effect produces supercurrent-controlled magnetization
hypotheses.result: Remanent flux patterns follow hysteresis loops controlled by bias current with ultra-low threshold

title: Absorption, dispersion, and emission characteristics of novel Chitosan blends thin films tuned by UV-Ozone
task: Tuning of optical, dielectric and nonlinear properties of Chitosan polymer blends via UV-Ozone treatment
key_results: Energy gap reduced from 5.14 eV (pristine) to 4.25 and 4 eV (blends); ε∞ reduced from 2.96 to 2.88 upon UV; fluorescence intensity decreases with UV exposure; third-order nonlinear susceptibility and nonlinear refractive index modified
comments: Comprehensive optical characterization, but the mechanistic connection between UV treatment and bandgap reduction remains undiscussed.

model_io.model: Spin-coated Chitosan and derivative polymer blend thin films

hypotheses.hypothesis: UV treatment modifies electronic structure and optical properties of Chitosan blends
hypotheses.result: Bandgap reduction and modified dielectric constants observed; fluorescence quenched

title: Evolution of tripartite entanglement in three-qubit quantum gravity-induced entanglement of masses (QGEM) with quantum decoherence
task: Evolution of tripartite entanglement in three-qubit QGEM system under decoherence
key_results: Three-qubit QGEM generates genuine tripartite entanglement; decoherence resilience improved vs two-qubit case; entanglement witness detection characterized; parameter spaces for genuine entanglement identified
comments: Addresses relevant decoherence concern for graviton-mediated entanglement tests, though practical implementation challenges for third mass remain.

model_io.model: Three-qubit QGEM system with gravitational interaction and environmental decoherence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Three-qubit QGEM generates and preserves genuine tripartite entanglement better than two-qubit setups under decoherence
hypotheses.result: Higher resilience to decoherence confirmed; genuine entanglement parameter space characterized

title: Coupled aerodynamic–electromagnetic modeling for RCS estimation of million-scale chaff clouds with arbitrarily curved 3D geometries
task: Coupled aerodynamic-electromagnetic RCS modeling of large-scale chaff clouds with arbitrary 3D geometries
key_results: Extended 6-DoF aerodynamic model handles flattened and helical dynamics; real-time RCS prediction for million-scale clouds; monostatic/bistatic RCS strongly influenced by aerodynamic orientation evolution; multi-frequency far-field enables Range-Doppler/Angle maps
comments: Physically comprehensive multiphysics framework with computational efficiency gains, though validation against real chaff deployment data would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: Coupled aerodynamic-electromagnetic system for chaff cloud RCS

hypotheses.hypothesis: Arbitrary 3D chaff geometries induce flattened and helical motions affecting RCS scattering signature
hypotheses.result: Coupled framework predicts strong RCS modulation by aerodynamic evolution; coexistence of flattened/helical motion critical to scattering response

title: A novel unified lightweight temporal-spatial transformer approach for intrusion detection in drone networks
task: Lightweight temporal-spatial transformer for intrusion detection in resource-constrained drone networks
key_results: 99.99% accuracy in multiclass attack detection; 100% binary anomaly detection; minimal footprint (0.04 MB, 9,722 parameters); evaluated on 2.3M labeled records from ISOT dataset
comments: Exceptional performance metrics on large dataset, though generalization to new drone platforms and attack types not addressed.

model_io.model: TSLT-Net: unified Temporal-Spatial Transformer with self-attention
inputs: network traffic data from drone networks
labeled attack/normal packets from ISOT Drone Anomaly Detection Dataset

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-attention mechanisms effectively capture temporal and spatial dependencies in drone network traffic for attack detection
hypotheses.result: TSLT-Net achieves 99.99% multiclass and 100% binary accuracy with minimal parameters
